[REASONING CONTEXT]
Phase: ACTION_PLANNING | Confidence: CALIBRATE | Trigger: STRUCTURE_ANALYSIS
Amplify: verify_genus_analysis; verify_hole_detection; verify_cycle_connectivity
Suppress: surface_treatment_only; topological_flattening
Cognitive Style: invariant_reasoning
[END REASONING CONTEXT]

## Analysis

**Step 1 вЂ” Map the graph (nodes, edges, connectivity)**

Containment (before refactoring):
```
Application A
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ M1 в†’ C1, C2
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ M2 в†’ C3, C4
в””в”Ђв”Ђ M3 в†’ C5
```

Dependency edges (directed):
| Source | Target | Kind |
|--------|--------|------|
| C1 | C3 | runtime instantiation (C1 must import C3's type в†’ **M1 imports M2**) |
| C3 | C5 | runtime instantiation (C3 must import C5's type в†’ **M2 imports M3**) |
| C5 | C1 | stored reference / DI (C5 must know C1's type в†’ **M3 imports M1**) |

**Step 2 вЂ” Identify structural invariants**

There is a **dependency cycle**: C1 в†’ C3 в†’ C5 в†’ C1, which at the module level is **M1 в†’ M2 в†’ M3 в†’ M1**. This cycle is a critical invariant вЂ” any cut across it will leave edges spanning the boundary in **both directions**.

**Step 3 вЂ” Apply the proposed extraction**

After moving M3 (C5) into Application B:

```
Application A          Application B
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ M1 в†’ C1, C2       в””в”Ђв”Ђ M3 в†’ C5
в””в”Ђв”Ђ M2 в†’ C3, C4
```

Cross-boundary dependencies that result:

| # | From | To | Direction | Type |
|---|------|----|-----------|------|
| 1 | M2 (App A) | M3 (App B) | A в†’ B | **Compile-time** import (C3 must know C5's type to instantiate it) |
| 2 | C3 (App A) | C5 (App B) | A в†’ B | **Runtime** instantiation |
| 3 | M3 (App B) | M1 (App A) | B в†’ A | **Compile-time** import (C5 must know C1's type to hold a reference) |
| 4 | C5 (App B) | C1 (App A) | B в†’ A | **Runtime** reference |

**Step 4 вЂ” Verdict**

**No.** The extraction cannot be done without cross-boundary dependencies. There are **2 compile-time cross-boundary module dependencies** and **2 runtime cross-application references**, totaling **4 cross-boundary dependencies**. The root cause is the dependency cycle M1 в†’ M2 в†’ M3 в†’ M1 вЂ” extracting any node from a cycle guarantees that edges will span the new boundary in both directions. You would need to break the cycle first (e.g., by introducing an interface/abstraction in App A that C5 depends on instead of the concrete C1) before a clean extraction is possible.
